evidence: The paper explicitly uses "permutation-based variable importance" to assess predictor contributions to organic carbon distribution. Authors state "surface and benthic salinity ranked the highest-importance predictors" and "our model ranked benthic primary production as a key predictor," interpreting variable rankings to explain hotspot drivers. These importance rankings are corroborated with partial dependence and ICE plots showing non-linear relationships. Since the impurity importance bias only affects Gini/MDI/variance-based importance measures, and this paper uses permutation-based importance instead, the p_affected concern does not apply.
